question.text: A 10-year GOI bond futures contract is traded at a clean price of Rs. 98.50 per Rs. 100 face value. What is the contract value?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Contract Value = Trade Price × 2000. Here, 98.50 × 2000 = Rs. 1,97,000. The unit of trading is 1 lot = 2000 bonds of face value Rs. 100 each (total notional FV = Rs. 2,00,000).
**The Trap:** Rs. 98,500 comes from multiplying by 1000 instead of 2000. Rs. 9,85,000 comes from multiplying by 10,000. Always remember: 1 lot = 2000 bonds.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Contract value calculations are fundamental to all derivative exams including NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives). The lot size of 2000 is specific to IRF.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The FV of one lot is Rs. 2 lakhs (2000 × Rs. 100). The contract value will be slightly less or more than Rs. 2 lakhs depending on whether the bond trades at discount or premium.
